title: Allocarya debilis
taxonomy: Allocarya debilis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. It belongs to the genus Allocarya, which is a group of plants native to South America. The family Boraginaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants that are commonly known as borage or forget-me-nots. The classification of Allocarya debilis reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in this family. This classification helps scientists understand the plant's characteristics and behaviors.

Its roots and leaves are often used to treat fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems due to their purported anti-inflammatory properties. intro: Allocarya debilis, also known as Western yarrow, is a perennial herb native to the western United States and Canada, characterized by its fern-like leaves and small white flowers. Traditionally, Western yarrow has been used to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable plant in natural medicine. The bioactive constituents of Western yarrow include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and volatile oils, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Western yarrow is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and salves to promote skin health and reduce inflammation.
